libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, environment variable, or system property is required. If tests manipulate Log4j global configuration, keep configuration files/resources test-local and reset or shut down the Log4j LoggerContext after the test to avoid cross-test state.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The artifact POM marks log4j-core as optional, so it is not guaranteed to be present from the normal scaffold, but Log4j documentation says the 1.2 bridge is used with log4j-api, log4j-core, and log4j-1.2-api. In 2.18.0, org.apache.log4j.config.PropertySetter converts Priority/Level via Log4j1Config
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #6772 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-1.2-api:2.18.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Optional Log4j integrations such as JMS, mail, Kafka, Jackson layouts, or async disruptor support have their own optional dependencies; do not exercise those paths unless the generated tests add the corresponding explicit dependencies.
Adding log4j-core changes tests from API-only bridge checks to real Log4j 2 runtime behavior, so assertions should avoid depending on ambient/global logging configuration.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: log4j-1.2-api is a Log4j 1.x bridge implemented on top of Log4j 2; version 2.18.0 now reaches classes that require log4j-core for normal compatibility behavior, including PropertySetter level conversion.
